reasoning: The question asks whether the US will accuse Russia of invading any country other than Ukraine before July 1, 2025. With a knowledge cutoff of May 1, 2025, there are only two months left in the forecasting period. Through multiple targeted searches of US government sites, news domains, and think tanks, there are no ongoing new invasions, nor any indicators of imminent invasions by Russia into other countries (such as Moldova, Georgia, or the Baltics) in 2024 or early 2025. Russia is currently fully occupied with its massive conventional war in Ukraine, recently mobilizing additional troops to sustain efforts there, which structurally limits their capacity to initiate a new invasion. Occasional troop movements in Africa (e.g., Niger) occurred with the consent of those governments and would not meet the resolution criteria of "without consent from the government". The base rate of a new invasion within a short two-month window is already exceedingly low (< 1%), and the lack of any precursors or intelligence warnings of an impending attack further depresses the probability. Therefore, the chance of the US accusing Russia of a new invasion before July 1, 2025, is assessed at the floor probability of 2%.
